channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Anti-poverty group says billionaires' wealth soared in 2024 as the elites prepare for another Davos
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Billionaires' wealth grew three times faster in 2024 than the year before, a top anti-poverty group reported on Monday as some of the world's political and financial elite prepared for an annual gathering in Davos, Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinians trek across rubble to return to their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes hold
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Even before the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas was fully in place on Sunday, Palestinians in the war-battered Gaza Strip began to return to the remains of the homes they had evacuated during the 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio will meet with peers from India, Japan, Australia after expected confirmation, officials say
item.description: U.S. officials say the Trump administration would begin formal international engagements this week when Marco Rubio — expected to be confirmed soon as secretary of state — meets with the foreign ministers of India, Japan and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Graham calls for the 'decimation' of Iranian nuclear program after ceasefire
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) on Sunday called for the “decimation” of the Iranian nuclear program in the wake of a new ceasefire in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Nigerian gasoline tanker explosion death toll rises to 86, with 55 others injured
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from a gasoline tanker explosion in north-central Nigeria has risen to 86, the country’s emergency response agency said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: A Colombian official says more than 80 people have been killed in the country’s northeast region following failed attempts by the government to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's impeached president is arrested over martial law declaration and his supporters riot
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hours after South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was formally arrested, triggering rioting by his supporters, his lawyers said Sunday that he remains defiant in his refusal to answer questions over the probe into his declaration of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban deputy tells leader there is no excuse for education bans on Afghan women and girls
item.description: A senior Taliban figure has urged the group's leader to scrap education bans on Afghan women and girls, saying there is no excuse for them, in a rare public rebuke of government policy.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 3 hostages under Israel cease-fire deal
item.description: Three female hostages were freed by Hamas under the terms of an Israeli cease-fire agreement, which was delayed by several hours Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US rejects Venezuelan President Maduro's reelection, but keeps financial lifeline for his government
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Things seemed to be looking up for Venezuela in 2022. Following years of authoritarian rule and withering economic sanctions, President Nicolás Maduro had agreed to work toward a democratic presidential election.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu praises Trump in 'temporary' ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu praised President-elect Trump for his role in negotiating a ceasefire between the nation and the militant group Hamas. “From the moment he was elected, President Trump has been involved in the mission for the release of our hostages.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — After more than a year of devastating war, the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is set to begin on Sunday, pausing the 15-month conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel Defense Forces will receive hostages Sunday with equipped camper trailers and comforting supplies
item.description: The Israel Defense forces completed preparations Saturday to receive 33 hostages being held by Hama Sunday after a cease-fire deal was reached.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 judges in Iran's capital tied to 1988 mass executions
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A man fatally shot two prominent hard-line judges in Iran's capital Saturday, officials said, both of whom allegedly took part in the mass execution of dissidents in 1988.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blessings for dogs? Bring them to Mexico City's cathedral and St. Anthony will do the rest
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Behaving at their best, a dozen dogs attended Mass at Mexico City’s cathedral Friday, waiting for their turn to be doused with holy water. The blessing of the animals is a long-time Catholic tradition celebrated on January 17.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daughter of one of the oldest Israeli hostages hopes for answers in ceasefire deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sharone Lifschitz is well aware that the odds are against her 84-year-old father. As one of the oldest hostages taken by Hamas, Oded Lifshitz would be among the first to be released under a ceasefire deal expected to begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP, citing the threat of Russian aggression
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuania has decided to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026 due to the threat of Russian aggression in the region, Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British PM Starmer visits Auschwitz, vows to fight antisemitism ahead of security talks in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday visited the Nazi German extermination camp at Auschwitz, voicing his “sheer horror” at what he saw there, before holding talks with Poland's leaders on stepping up European defense and tightening Britain's ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 lawyers for the late Kremlin foe Alexei Navalny are convicted and sentenced to prison
item.description: PETUSHKI, Russia (AP) — Three lawyers who once represented the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny were convicted by a court Friday as part of the Kremlin’s crackdown on dissent that has reached levels unseen since Soviet times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Iran sign a partnership treaty to deepen their ties in the face of Western sanctions
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a broad cooperation pact Friday as their countries deepened their partnership in the face of stinging Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, Hamas cease-fire held up over renewed debate over Philadelphi security corridor, terrorist exchange
item.description: Old disputes over security concerns involving the Philadelphi corridor have apparently stalled a deal that Israel was set to vote on after the U.S. and Qatar said hostage exchanges would begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American college rape suspect is being extradited to the US
item.description: Ian Cleary, who is accused of sexually assaulting a Gettysburg College student at a party in 2013, is being sent back to the U.S., officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
